description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white surface, interspersed with gray and brown veining and patches. The rock has a rough, natural texture with visible mineral striations and subtle color gradients, suggesting it is composed mainly of quartz or marble. Its surface is uneven, with some flatter areas and several protruding edges, giving it a rugged appearance. The coloration varies from creamy white to darker gray and brown, especially near the base and in crevices. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its overall form and coloration make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A dense, irregular white rock with gray and brown veining, rough texture, and natural mineral striations.
description_view_0: This view shows a prominent white face with gray and brown marbling, and a rounded, slightly sloped top.
description_view_1: The rock appears flatter and more elongated, with lighter coloration and subtle surface cracks visible.
description_view_2: A more angular perspective, highlighting the rock's uneven edges and a mix of white and gray tones.
description_view_3: This side displays a broad, mostly white surface with some darker patches and a less pronounced texture.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
